title: analytics — kill switches + rollout controls
description: Per-provider kill switches let ops flip a bad provider off in seconds without a redeploy. All kill switches default ON.

flags.description: Master analytics capture kill switch. Off = ALL analytics capture is skipped (event never persisted, no downstream fan-out). Used during ingest incidents.
Fan-out dispatch kill switch. Off = events still persisted BUT no HTTP calls fire. Backlog processes when re-enabled (queued dispatch jobs pick up pending events).
Disable PostHog dispatch immediately. Failover: other configured providers still receive events.
Disable identity-merge processing globally. Used when MergeAliasedEventsJob backlog explodes during a bulk identify() rollout.
Disable batch flushing. Non-batching providers keep working. Used when a batching provider's rate limits are hit and we need to slow down globally.
Strict PII regex scan in PropertyValidator. Off = warnings instead of refusals. Should always be on in production; only disable for a debug session with super_admin approval.
Master session-recording rollout gate. Combined with analytics_session_recording entitlement + subject-level functional consent. All three must be true for a recording to fire.
